title: viburnum rafinesqueanum var. affine
intro: Viburnum rafinesqueanum var. affine, also known as Downy Arrowwood, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its small white flowers and dark blue berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Downy Arrowwood include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and act as a natural antihistamine to alleviate allergic reactions. Downy Arrowwood can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and berries in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
